a: Values that describe errors the AccessoryAccess framework returns.
Values that represent error codes that the AccessoryAccess framework returns.
An error code that indicates there’s already an accessory listener for the USB accessory.
An error code that indicates the USB accessory isn’t accessible since it may already be in use.
An error value that represents an internal error.
An error value that indicates the accessory isn’t in the correct state for the current operation.
The string that represents the framework’s error domain.
A class that represents a USB accessory.
Events that represent accessory connection and disconnection.
The event the framework sends when a USB accessory connects to the system.
The method the framework calls when a USB accessory disconnects from the system.
A class that conforms to the framework’s USB accessory listener protocol can listen to the accessory events.
A class your app uses to manage USB accessories and the listener objects for those accessories.
A class that represents the accessory matching criteria for a USB accessory.
A structure you provide that enumerates which device characteristics to search for.
A structure you provide that enumerates which device interface characteristics to search for.
Values that represent options for performing interface matching using interface matching criteria.
A value that indicates the match needs to be all inclusive of the provided interface criteria.
A value that indicates that the match needs to cover at least one of the provided interface criteria.
Manage access to connected USB accessories.
An error that indicates the accessory listener is already registered, and therefore the app can’t re-register it.
An error that indicates the USB accessory isn’t accessible.
The 2-byte Binary-Coded Decimal (BCD) value defined by the manufacturer to indicate the device revision or version number.
Closes all connections to the USB accessory for this process.
Returns the currently selected configuration descriptor data.
The value that represents the configuration.
Creates an encoded representation of the USB accessory.
The value that represents the device class.
Returns the device descriptor data.
The value that represents the device protocol.
The value that represents the device subclass.
A value that represents the Accessory Access framework’s error domain.
Returns an asynchronous list of events that match the provided criteria.
Creates a USB accessory from an XPC representation.
Creates a new USB accessory with the provided coder.
Initializes a criteria object with the provided USB device matching properties.
Initializes a criteria object using USB interface matching criteria, for the given USB device matching criteria.
Initializes a criteria object using a USB device matching dictionary.
Initializes a criteria object using USB interface matching dictionaries, for the provided USB device matching criteria.
Creates a new error code with the provided value.
Initializes a new interface matching option with the provided value.
Initializes a new matching criteria structure with the provided values.
Initializes a new device matching criteria structure with the provided values.
Creates a USB accessory from an XPC representation.
The value that represents the interface class.
The value that represents the interface number.
The value that represents the interface protocol.
The value that represents the interface subclass.
An error that represents an internal error.
An error that indicates the accessory isn’t in the correct state for the current operation.
Opens a connection to the USB accessory for this process to access it exclusively.
The value that represents the product ID.
The value that represents the product ID.
Registers a USB accessory listener.
Returns the IORegistry ID for the USB accessory.
Returns the shared USB accessory manager object for this process.
The value that represents the device speed.
The value that represents the interface speed.
Unregister a previously registered listener.
The method the framework invokes when a USB accessory connects to the system.
The method the framework invokes when a USB accessory disconnects from the system.
The value that represents the vendor ID.
The value that represents the vendor ID.
